AM.T: And God said, “Let there be light,” and there was light.

PM.T: But Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”

AM.T: He who trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous will thrive like foliage.

PM.T: For our light and momentary affliction is producing for us an eternal weight of glory that is far beyond comparison.

AM.T: Blessed is the man who finds wisdom, the man who acquires understanding,

PM.T: Live in freedom, but do not use your freedom as a cover-up for evil; live as servants of God.

AM.T: Oil and incense bring joy to the heart, and the counsel of a friend is sweetness to the soul.

PM.T: Do everything in love.

AM.T: God is our refuge and strength, an ever-present help in times of trouble.

PM.T: In Him we were also chosen as God’s own, having been predestined according to the plan of Him who works out everything by the counsel of His will,

AM.T: The Law of the LORD is perfect, reviving the soul;

PM.T: My brothers, as you hold out your faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, do not show favoritism.

AM.T: When all has been heard, the conclusion of the matter is this: Fear God and keep His commandments, because this is the whole duty of man.

PM.T: Above all, my brothers, do not swear, not by heaven or earth or by any other oath. Simply let your “Yes” be yes, and your “No,” no, so that you will not fall under judgment.

AM.T: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

PM.T: For you have been born again, not of perishable seed, but of imperishable, through the living and enduring word of God.

AM.T: He will again have compassion on us; He will vanquish our iniquities. You will cast out all our sins into the depths of the sea.

PM.T: I and the Father are one.”

AM.T: For His anger is fleeting, but His favor lasts a lifetime. Weeping may stay the night, but joy comes in the morning.

PM.T: And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.

AM.T: The earth is the LORD’s, and the fullness thereof, the world and all who dwell therein.

PM.T: Therefore you are no longer strangers and foreigners, but fellow citizens with the saints and members of God’s household,

AM.T: May the LORD our God be with us, as He was with our fathers. May He never leave us or forsake us.

PM.T: And we have this commandment from Him: Whoever loves God must love his brother as well.

AM.T: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; may Your glory cover all the earth.

PM.T: And a voice from heaven said, “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased!”

AM.T: The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in Him, and I am helped. Therefore my heart rejoices, and I give thanks to Him with my song.

PM.T: Therefore, just as you have received Christ Jesus as Lord, continue to walk in Him,

AM.T: Remember what happened long ago, for I am God, and there is no other; I am God, and there is none like Me.

PM.T: For the word of God is living and active. Sharper than any double-edged sword, it pierces even to dividing soul and spirit, joints and marrow. It judges the thoughts and intentions of the heart.

AM.T: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; a broken and a contrite heart, O God, You will not despise.

PM.T: But you shall not be like them. Instead, the greatest among you should be like the youngest, and the one who leads like the one who serves.

AM.T: For the LORD gives wisdom; from His mouth come knowledge and understanding.

PM.T: “If anyone comes to Me and does not hate his father and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters — yes, even his own life — he cannot be My disciple.

AM.T: Kindness to the poor is a loan to the LORD, and He will repay the lender.

PM.T: Now may the God of peace, who through the blood of the eternal covenant brought back from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great Shepherd of the sheep, equip you with every good thing to do His will.

AM.T: You have given me Your shield of salvation, and Your gentleness exalts me.

PM.T: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will never pass away.

AM.T: But you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, who are small among the clans of Judah, out of you will come forth for Me One to be ruler over Israel — One whose origins are of old, from the days of eternity.

PM.T: “I am the true vine, and My Father is the keeper of the vineyard.

AM.T: Blessed is the one You choose and bring near to dwell in Your courts!

PM.T: This is a trustworthy saying, worthy of full acceptance: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am the worst.

AM.T: Do not answer a fool according to his folly, or you yourself will be like him. Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he become wise in his own eyes.

PM.T: In My Father’s house are many rooms. If it were not so, would I have told you that I am going there to prepare a place for you?

AM.T: May Your loving devotion rest on us, O LORD, as we put our hope in You.

PM.T: Therefore, holy brothers, who share in the heavenly calling, set your focus on Jesus, the apostle and high priest whom we confess.

AM.T: As for God, His way is perfect; the word of the LORD is flawless. He is a shield to all who take refuge in Him.

PM.T: and walk in love, just as Christ loved us and gave Himself up for us as a fragrant sacrificial offering to God.

AM.T: Not to us, O LORD, not to us, but to Your name be the glory, because of Your loving devotion, because of Your faithfulness.

PM.T: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost.”

AM.T: Where can I go to escape Your Spirit? Where can I flee from Your presence?

PM.T: Anyone, then, who knows the right thing to do, yet fails to do it, is guilty of sin.

AM.T: O God, You are my God. Earnestly I seek You; my soul thirsts for You. My body yearns for You in a dry and weary land without water.

PM.T: Love takes no pleasure in evil, but rejoices in the truth.

AM.T: Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.

PM.T: Those I love I rebuke and discipline. Therefore be earnest and repent.

AM.T: Behold, how good and pleasant it is when brothers live together in harmony!

PM.T: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision has any value. What matters is faith expressing itself through love.

AM.T: And God looked upon all that He had made, and indeed, it was very good. And there was evening, and there was morning — the sixth day.

PM.T: This is the message you have heard from the beginning: We should love one another.

AM.T: Many plans are in a man’s heart, but the purpose of the LORD will prevail.

PM.T: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.
